item.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Robinhood files confidentially for its second venture fund, this time targeting growth as well as early-stage startups.

item.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

item.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In an email to beta testers, the company said the site's goal is to "track the most influential voices in a space" and to surface the news that's actually worth "paying attention to."

item.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

item.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

item.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

item.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

item.title: Voice AI in India is hard — Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

item.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
item.dc:creator: Natasha Lomas, Romain Dillet, Kyle Wiggers, Lucas Ropek
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

item.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

item.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

item.title: The “people’s airline” and the enterprise AI gold rush
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Everyone wants a piece of the enterprise AI pie, and this week, we saw a string of companies making their moves. From Anthropic and OpenAI announcing new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment to SAP dropping $1B on German AI startup Prior Labs, it’s becoming clear that if you’re a startup building enterprise tools, you’re likely an acquisition target.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, hosts Kirsten Korosec, Anthony […]

item.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

item.title: The fax machine is the bottleneck in US healthcare, and VCs are starting to notice
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. For now, the founders say the administrative staff they work with aren't worried about that; they're more worried about drowning.

item.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The new features could be handy for customer service systems, but OpenAI says they have applications that work across a variety of other fields, including education and creator platforms.

item.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: AI startup Pit is led by the co-founders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

item.title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The company is expanding its efforts to protect ChatGPT users in cases where conversations may turn to self-harm.

item.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer is now available to everyone on Mac
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Perplexity's Personal Computer brings AI agents to your Mac, and is now open to everyone.
